label: Expert systems, production rules & rule engines

node.label: Expert systems, production rules & rule engines
node.definition: Expert and production-rule systems apply explicit if–then rules over symbolic facts, separating a curated knowledge base from an inference engine.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Expert systems, production rules & rule engines is to connect Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas, Constraint propagation, logic & program execution to downstream capabilities including Knowledge graphs, semantic search & relational reasoning, Verified and assurance-gated agents.
examples: substrate for Knowledge graphs, semantic search & relational reasoning
substrate for Verified and assurance-gated agents
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas, Constraint propagation, logic & program execution.

dependencies.rationale: Expert systems, production rules & rule engines consumes, emits, or organizes information using Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas.
Expert systems, production rules & rule engines is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Constraint propagation, logic & program execution.
Expert systems, production rules & rule engines is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Boolean circuits & differentiable logic gates.
